skill.name: developer-growth-analysis
skill.description: Analyzes recent Claude Code chat history to identify development patterns, improvement areas, and learning resources, then can deliver the report to Slack.
skill.summary: Review recent coding chats and create a personalized developer growth report.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static external-command and weak-cryptography alerts are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline backticks, and ordinary prose. Real concerns remain: the skill instructs access to local Claude history, including pasted content, and sends a derived report through Slack using Rube MCP.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Local Chat History Access
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs the assistant to read ~/.claude/history.jsonl and process display, project, timestamp, and pastedContents fields. This is disclosed, but it can expose private prompts, project names, and pasted code from local Claude Code sessions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path and specific fields are explicitly listed. The privacy risk is direct because pastedContents may contain source code or secrets.

medium_findings.title: External Slack Delivery of Derived Developer Data
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill tells the assistant to send the complete growth report to Slack DMs through Rube MCP. The report is derived from local chat history, so delivery can move sensitive work details to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Slack delivery flow is explicit and includes connection checks, authorization, and message sending. It is user-facing but still creates third-party data exposure.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Resource Searches Based on Work Patterns
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill uses Rube MCP to search HackerNews for each improvement area. Search queries based on technologies, debugging topics, and gaps may reveal sensitive project context to an external tool.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external search behavior is explicit. The exact sensitivity depends on generated queries, but the source data is the user's private work history.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static external_commands findings correspond to Markdown fenced examples, output templates, and inline formatting. No executable Ruby or shell backtick code is present in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown examples and templates, not runnable source files. No command invocation syntax or script interpreter context was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Prose Matches
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static weak-cryptography alerts occur in ordinary descriptive text and example report content. No evidence of cryptographic algorithms, hashing, encryption, or credential processing was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines shows report prose and example learning-resource text. There is no code implementing MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak primitives.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alert Is Development Self-Assessment
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The scanner flagged line 79, but the text asks the assistant to identify challenges in the user's coding work. It does not enumerate host, network, account, or environment reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is developer growth analysis from chat history. No evidence of system inventory, privilege checks, network discovery, or persistence probing was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Reads Hidden Claude History File
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs access to a hidden local Claude history file that can contain private requests, project context, and pasted content.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact hidden path and sensitive fields are named in the instructions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sends Report to Slack Through MCP
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the assistant to use Rube MCP to send the complete report to Slack direct messages.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Slack send workflow is explicit and operationally described.

content.user_title: Analyze Developer Growth from Recent Chats
content.value_statement: Developers often lack structured feedback from their daily AI-assisted work. This skill reviews recent Claude Code chats, identifies growth areas, and creates a focused learning report.
actual_capabilities: Reads recent Claude Code chat history from a local JSONL file.
Identifies projects, technologies, problem types, and recurring challenges.
Ranks three to five improvement areas using evidence from recent chats.
Creates a structured developer growth report with strengths and action items.
Searches HackerNews for learning resources related to improvement areas.
Can send the completed report to the user's Slack direct messages.
limitations: Requires access to local Claude Code chat history.
May expose sensitive work details when Slack delivery or external search is used.
Resource quality depends on available HackerNews results.
Findings are guidance, not a substitute for code review or mentoring.

use_cases.title: Weekly Skill Review
use_cases.description: Create a short growth report from recent coding chats and choose one focus area for the next week.

use_cases.title: Mentorship Preparation
use_cases.description: Summarize recent challenges, strengths, and learning needs before a mentoring session or career check-in.

use_cases.title: Learning Plan Curation
use_cases.description: Connect recurring technical gaps to relevant articles and discussions for targeted study.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Growth Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a default report.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my developer growth from my recent Claude Code chats and give me a concise report.

prompt_templates.title: Today Focus Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Review work from a specific short period.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my coding work from today. Highlight repeated challenges, strengths, and the top three areas to improve.

prompt_templates.title: Technology-Specific Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Focus the analysis on one stack or project type.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my recent chats for TypeScript and React work. Identify patterns, gaps, and learning resources for those areas.

prompt_templates.title: Mentor-Ready Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced report for sharing with another person.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a mentor-ready growth report from the last 48 hours of chats. Include evidence, priorities, action items, and Slack delivery only after I approve the final text.

output_examples.input: Analyze my developer growth from my recent chats.
output_examples.output: A report summarizing recent projects, common technologies, three prioritized improvement areas, observed strengths, and practical next actions.

output_examples.input: Focus on my debugging habits from today.
output_examples.output: A focused review showing repeated debugging patterns, likely knowledge gaps, stronger habits to keep, and suggested resources for deeper practice.

output_examples.input: Prepare a learning plan from my last two days of work.
output_examples.output: A study plan with improvement themes, time estimates, curated article topics, and a short checklist for the next week.

best_practices: Review the report before sending it to Slack or sharing it externally.
Remove secrets, private customer details, and proprietary code from pasted chat content.
Run the analysis on a regular cadence so trends are easier to compare.
anti_patterns: Do not use the report as the only measure of engineering performance.
Do not send reports to Slack without checking sensitive project details first.
Do not treat generic article recommendations as proof of a real skill gap.

faq.question: What data does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: It analyzes recent Claude Code chat history, including messages, project names, timestamps, and pasted content.

faq.question: Does it run code from my projects?
faq.answer: No evidence found that it runs project code. The file contains analysis instructions and Markdown examples.

faq.question: Can it send information outside my machine?
faq.answer: Yes. It can search HackerNews through Rube MCP and send the report to Slack direct messages.

faq.question: Should I review the report before Slack delivery?
faq.answer: Yes. Review it first because it may include sensitive project details or summaries of pasted code.

faq.question: How often should I use it?
faq.answer: Weekly use is a good fit because it gives enough recent work to identify patterns.

faq.question: Is this a replacement for code review?
faq.answer: No. It is a personal learning aid and should complement code review, mentoring, and direct project feedback.
